verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is a secreted cytokine with no intrinsic TM domain or GPI anchor. However, on activated Tregs, platelets, megakaryocytes, and stimulated B cells, the latent TGF-β1 complex (via LAP) forms a covalent disulfide linkage with the transmembrane protein GARP (LRRC32), tethering it stably to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. This covalent, wash-resistant GARP:TGF-β1 complex is detected by flow cytometry on the cell surface and is the target of clinical-stage anti-GARP:TGF-β1 monoclonal antibody programs. A parallel LRRC33-dependent surface tethering occurs on myeloid cells. Surface display is strictly cell-type and activation-state dependent — absent in most resting cells where TGF-β1 is secreted into the ECM/soluble pool. Verdict: contextual / stable_surface_attachment via covalent disulfide tethering to TM scaffold GARP.
